MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION (TRANSACTION ID # 16040024) FILED BY PLAINTIFF ANTE, ALEJANDRO , AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MACY'S WEST STORES, INC. AND MACY'S INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND DISMISS OR, ALTERNATIVELY, STAY CIVIL PROCEEDINGS, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND THE CASE IS STAYED PENDING COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO SEVER AND DECLARE UNENFORCEABLE THE PROVISIONS OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT THAT MAKES THE AMERICAN ARBITRATION ASSOCIATION THE ARBITRATION PROVIDER IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN THAT THE PROVISIONS MAKING THE AAA THE ARBITRATION PROVIDER IS UNCONSCIONABLE BASED ON THE CURRENT RECORD BEFORE THE COURT, IF FURTHER FACTS ARE DEVELOPED DURING THE COURSE OF THE PROCESS OF SELECTING THE ARBITRATOR ESTABLISH THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT GET A "FAIR ARBITRATION" DUE TO THE SO-CALLED "REPEAT PLAYER EFFECT, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A MOTION TO VACATE THE ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION. WITHOUT MORE, THE "REPEAT PLAYER EFFECT" IS INSUFFICIENT TO RENDER AN AGREEMENT PROVISION UNCONSCIONABLE. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUBMITTED EVIDENCE WHICH DEMONSTRATES THAT THE PROSPECTIVE AAA ARBITRATORS AVAILABLE FOR THIS CASE ARE UNDULY LIMITED OR THAT SOME OR ALL OF THEM ARE LIKELY BIASED IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANTS DUE TO THE REPEAT PLAYER EFFECT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR #11185 (302/HK)

Off Calendar Apr-12-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay Civil Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANTS MACY'S WEST STORES, INC. AND MACY'S INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Dismiss Or, Alternatively, Stay Civil Proceedings, Defendants' motion to compel arbitration is granted and the case is stayed pending completion of the arbitration proceedings. Plaintiff's request to sever and declare unenforceable the provisions of the parties' agreement that makes the American Arbitration Association the arbitration provider is denied. Although plaintiff has not shown that the provisions making the AAA the arbitration provider is unconscionable based on the current record before the court, if further facts are developed during the course of the process of selecting the arbitrator establish that plaintiff cannot get a "fair arbitration" due to the so-called "repeat player effect, plaintiff may file a motion to vacate the order compelling arbitration. Without more, the "repeat player effect" is insufficient to render an agreement provision unconscionable. Plaintiff has not submitted evidence which demonstrates that the prospective AAA arbitrators available for this case are unduly limited or that some or all of them are likely biased in favor of defendants due to the repeat player effect. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR #11185 (302/HK)
